triage.rationale: Code matches: handle_host does `if (len <= BLOB_LIMIT)` then `memcpy(host, val, len)` into char host[128]; BLOB_LIMIT guard does not prevent overflow for 129<=len<=4096; reachable via decode on CLI file input.

Code matches: handle_slice checks only `if (off > stream_len)` then `memcpy(window, stream + off, n)` with n=min(want,512); missing upper bound off+n <= stream_len allows OOB read past input buffer; reachable from T_SLICE fields.

Code matches: handle_table computes total=rows*width (u32), sizes vector to (possibly wrapped) total, then loops `for(i=0; i < (size_t)rows*width; i++) table[i]=...`; large rows/width from T_TABLE produce undersized alloc + OOB write; direct from decode.

Code matches: handle_drop does `delete ctx.pending;` with no nulling; handle_flush dereferences if non-null, decode-end does final delete, and later STAGE does delete; T_STAGE+T_DROP sequences from untrusted fields reach UAF/double-free.

Code matches: handle_append does `first = &ctx.log.back();` after first push_back then more push_back for len>1, followed by `first->tag ^= ...`; vector reallocation on subsequent pushes invalidates the pointer; T_APPEND len controls it.

Code matches: handle_check has `assert(len <= sizeof(scratch))` then `memcpy(scratch, val, len)` into uint8_t[32]; assert is not a release guard so len>32 from T_CHECK overflows stack; reachable from decode.
Duplicate of RECON-3a2baba6a151f4b7: identical stack buffer overflow in handle_host using wrong BLOB_LIMIT guard.
Duplicate of RECON-68af280a3848ed75: identical missing off+n bound check in handle_slice OOB read.
Duplicate of RECON-7eb49c293e68a822: identical uint32 overflow in handle_table allocation vs fill loop.
Duplicate of RECON-e87842bdb40ab26a: identical assert-only guard in handle_check stack overflow.
Duplicate of RECON-99b87eb62f4c4868: identical missing null after delete in handle_drop leading to UAF/double-free.
